topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper seemed to vibrate under my gaze. A C-minus. After pulling an all-nighter, sacrificing sleep, and meticulously reviewing every chapter, this was the reward? I slammed my textbook shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet library corner. My jaw tightened until a dull ache radiated across my temples. I didn't bother to look at the professor; I just shoved the graded paper into my backpack with unnecessary force, the zipper catching with a grating sound. Every muscle in my body felt coiled, vibrating with a sharp, hot resentment. I walked out of the building without acknowledging anyone, the campus air feeling suddenly too thin, too sharp.

[story 2]

"Unbelievable." The word escaped my lips, low and ragged. I stared at the score, my breath catching in a sudden, shallow gasp. It was a catastrophic failure of my effort, a complete dismissal of the hours I’d poured into this assignment. My hands clenched into fists so tight my knuckles bleached white. I could feel the heat rising in my chest, a pressure building behind my ribs that made breathing difficult. I paced my small dorm room, running a hand roughly through my already messy hair. I kicked a discarded water bottle across the floor, the sound sharp and brittle. This wasn't just a bad grade; it felt like a personal slight, a deliberate slight against my intelligence.

[story 3]

I picked up the transcript, my fingers trembling slightly as I scanned the disappointing figure. A deep, simmering anger began to bubble up, hot and viscous. I reread the feedback, each sentence a deliberate assault on my sense of competence. A slow, deliberate movement took over me; I methodically stacked the papers on my desk, aligning them with unnerving precision. My voice, when I finally spoke, was dangerously quiet, a low monotone that held an edge of pure, controlled irritation. "This is unacceptable," I managed, the words tasting like ash in my mouth. I stood up, the movement stiff, and walked toward the door, determined to burn the whole semester down just to feel something other than this crushing disappointment.
